scope: Independent verification of the Rust broker server, config/reload semantics, cancellation lifecycle, provider boundary, addon boundary, postprocessing path, and test quality for the MVP-030 slice.

summary.result: Broker/server/addon artifacts are trusted for the MVP server/addons slice. No blocking defects were found.
trusted_artifacts: crates/autocomplete-core provider/postprocessing integration used by the server
crates/autocomplete-provider-pi as a broker-selected provider dependency only
non_blocking_notes: No editor clients exist yet in this workspace, so client/provider-pi separation can only be verified as absence of client artifacts plus protocol/core/addon dependency cleanliness. MVP-041 should re-check once clients are added.

Cancellation registration starts after JSON validation and addon preparation. Those steps are cheap and deterministic now, but a cancel request racing before start_request can return not_found. This is acceptable for the current MVP slice, not a blocker.
Completed request_id tracking is in-memory and unbounded, matching MVP-030's known limitation. It is not a verification failure for this slice.

commands_run.result: passed. Full workspace test suite passed: addon, core, protocol, provider-pi, and server tests/doc-tests.

commands_run.command: cargo run -q -p autocomplete-server --bin autocomplete-broker -- --config <temp mock config>; curl /health; curl /v1/autocomplete
commands_run.result: passed. /health returned protocol_version 1, status ok, provider mock/available. /v1/autocomplete against examples/fixtures/autocomplete-request.v1.json returned status ok, insert_text '"Ada")', confidence 0.93, source mock, metadata.postprocessed true.

commands_run.command: grep for autocomplete-provider-pi/provider symbols across Cargo.toml and source files
commands_run.result: passed. autocomplete-provider-pi is imported only by autocomplete-server production code and provider-pi tests; protocol/core/addons do not depend on provider-pi. No clients directory exists yet.

evidence: crates/autocomplete-server/src/lib.rs exposes GET /health, POST /v1/autocomplete, POST /v1/cancel/{request_id}, and POST /v1/reload via axum routes.
Live broker smoke with mock provider returned expected /health and /v1/autocomplete responses.

crates/autocomplete-server/tests/http_endpoints.rs covers health, valid autocomplete, semantic invalid request rejection, cancel lifecycle, reload, pi health selection, privacy rejection, context-byte-limit rejection, and Obsidian-style Markdown requests.
BrokerConfig loads TOML with serde defaults and deny_unknown_fields; validate rejects zero context window, zero remote context byte limit, and zero idle delay.
provider_from_config switches between mock and pi solely from BrokerConfig.provider.kind.
reload_config reloads from the original config path and swaps BrokerRuntime under the runtime RwLock.
config_loading.rs and http_endpoints.rs verify partial TOML defaults, pi selection in health, and reload changing mock output without client/protocol changes.
The protocol crate has no provider-pi dependency and grep found no provider-pi/pi-provider symbols in crates/autocomplete-protocol.

Cargo dependency graph keeps provider-pi at the server composition layer: autocomplete-server depends on autocomplete-provider-pi; autocomplete-core and autocomplete-addons depend only on protocol/core abstractions.

PiProvider command construction includes --mode json, --no-tools, --no-session, --no-context-files, --no-extensions, --no-skills, --no-prompt-templates, --no-themes, model, thinking, and system prompt arguments; pi provider tests assert the isolation flags with a fake executable.
No editor clients are present yet, so no client can currently import provider-pi. This must be re-verified when MVP-040 adds clients.
AddonRuntime.prepare applies request-supplied context trimming, enforces privacy policy, and selects a prompt before the server calls the provider.
AddonRuntime.postprocessor_pipeline supplies the broker's postprocessor pipeline to AutocompleteEngine at BrokerRuntime construction.
Built-in addons do not read workspace files; NearbyTextWindow only trims prefix/suffix already supplied by the client, and PolicyEngine only matches the request document URI/context byte counts.
AutocompleteEngine processes every ProviderOutput::Candidate through self.postprocessors.process before constructing an Ok response.
Rejected or empty postprocessed candidates become NoSuggestion rather than leaking raw provider text.
Live smoke and server tests assert metadata.postprocessed true for successful candidate responses.
AppState tracks in-flight request_ids with CancellationToken values; cancel_request cancels in-flight tokens and reports already_completed after finish_request.
AutocompleteEngine selects over cancellation and request deadline while awaiting provider completion, and MockProvider/PiProvider observe cancellation tokens.
http_endpoints.rs verifies cancellation of a slow mock autocomplete and subsequent already_completed status.
cancellation.note: Cancel-before-registration can return not_found because start_request occurs after validation/addon preparation. Current preparation is cheap; not a blocker.

strengths: Tests cover public HTTP routes and semantic response bodies, not only unit internals.
Provider-pi subprocess behavior is fake-tested for isolation flags, JSONL parsing, repair retry, timeout, cancellation, and redaction.
Protocol fixtures/schema validation and server endpoint tests exercise the same JSON fixtures used by smoke tests.
Privacy policy and postprocessing are tested at both addon/core and server-integration levels.
gaps_not_blocking: No live real-pi smoke or latency measurement; explicitly deferred to MVP-050.
No concurrent duplicate request_id stress test; existing start_request logic cancels previous tokens but is not stress-tested.
No client boundary tests yet because client artifacts are not present.

complexity_and_boundaries.assessment: The design stays within acceptable MVP complexity: protocol/core/provider/addon/server crates have clear directions of dependency, server composes providers/addons, and provider-specific code is not in protocol/core/addons. The addon registries are intentionally simple built-ins rather than premature dynamic plugin loading.

queue_recommendation: Proceed to MVP-040 editor clients. Re-check provider boundary once clients exist, and leave real pi runtime latency/model smoke to MVP-050.
